title: Todd Duffee

infobox.value: [American Top Team](/wiki/American_Top_Team "American Top Team") (until 2009)[[2]](#cite_note-SHERDOG-2)   [Grudge Training Center](/wiki/Grudge_Training_Center "Grudge Training Center") (formerly)[[3]](#cite_note-cotv12-3)   Combat Sports Academy[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]   [Xtreme Couture](/wiki/Xtreme_Couture "Xtreme Couture") (2009–present)[[4]](#cite_note-fm09-4)   [American Kickboxing Academy](/wiki/American_Kickboxing_Academy "American Kickboxing Academy") (2011–present)[[3]](#cite_note-cotv12-3)

(born December 6, 1985) is an American [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") who competes in the [Heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight_(MMA) "Heavyweight (MMA)") division of [Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki](/wiki/Konfrontacja_Sztuk_Walki "Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki") (KSW). He trains predominantly with the Combat Sports Academy[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] and the [American Kickboxing Academy](/wiki/American_Kickboxing_Academy "American Kickboxing Academy") in California. Duffee has also competed in the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [Super Fight League](/wiki/Super_Fight_League "Super Fight League") in India, [Jungle Fight](/wiki/Jungle_Fight "Jungle Fight") in Brazil, and [Dynamite!! 2010](/wiki/Dynamite!!_2010 "Dynamite!! 2010") in Japan. In addition to his mixed martial arts career he had a role in the 2011 film *[Never Back Down 2: The Beatdown](/wiki/Never_Back_Down_2:_The_Beatdown "Never Back Down 2: The Beatdown")* as well as minor roles in both film and television. Duffee has also appeared on the front cover of *[Muscle & Fitness](/wiki/Muscle_%26_Fitness "Muscle & Fitness")* magazine.

**Todd Duffee** (born December 6, 1985) is an American [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") who competes in the [Heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight_(MMA) "Heavyweight (MMA)") division of [Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki](/wiki/Konfrontacja_Sztuk_Walki "Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki") (KSW). He trains predominantly with the Combat Sports Academy[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] and the [American Kickboxing Academy](/wiki/American_Kickboxing_Academy "American Kickboxing Academy") in California. Duffee has also competed in the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"), [Super Fight League](/wiki/Super_Fight_League "Super Fight League") in India, [Jungle Fight](/wiki/Jungle_Fight "Jungle Fight") in Brazil, and [Dynamite!! 2010](/wiki/Dynamite!!_2010 "Dynamite!! 2010") in Japan. In addition to his mixed martial arts career he had a role in the 2011 film *[Never Back Down 2: The Beatdown](/wiki/Never_Back_Down_2:_The_Beatdown "Never Back Down 2: The Beatdown")* as well as minor roles in both film and television. Duffee has also appeared on the front cover of *[Muscle & Fitness](/wiki/Muscle_%26_Fitness "Muscle & Fitness")* magazine.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Duffee was born in [Evansville, Indiana](/wiki/Evansville,_Indiana "Evansville, Indiana"), but grew up in [Raleigh, Illinois](/wiki/Raleigh,_Illinois "Raleigh, Illinois"), and [Eldorado](/wiki/Eldorado,_Illinois "Eldorado, Illinois") as the third son of a coal miner father and nurse mother. Duffee played many sports growing up, including baseball, football, track, bull riding, soccer, and basketball. Duffee also began training in [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") but was not very devoted to it as he was playing other sports.[[5]](#cite_note-mmaj09-5) A standout football player, he was signed on to play college football but his career was derailed from an injury. Duffee then moved to [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta"), Georgia, when he was 18 years old and continued boxing, before watching a UFC event and shifting his training to mixed martial arts.[[5]](#cite_note-mmaj09-5) He was working on a degree from the [University of Georgia](/wiki/University_of_Georgia "University of Georgia") before transferring to [University of Nevada, Las Vegas](/wiki/University_of_Nevada,_Las_Vegas "University of Nevada, Las Vegas"), then dropping out to train mixed martial arts full-time.[[2]](#cite_note-SHERDOG-2)[[4]](#cite_note-fm09-4) As of February 2016, he is pursuing a degree in business from the University of Georgia.
[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Mixed martial arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Duffee won his first three fights against Mike Walbright, Mark Haniger and Josh Bennett. Duffee then agreed to fight UFC and [PRIDE FC](/wiki/PRIDE_FC "PRIDE FC") veteran, [Assuério Silva](/wiki/Assu%C3%A9rio_Silva "Assuério Silva") in the main event of [Jungle Fights](/wiki/Jungle_Fight "Jungle Fight") 11. Duffee dominated the fight and won by [knockout](/wiki/Knockout "Knockout") in the second round.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Duffee was set to make his UFC debut against [Mostapha al-Turk](/wiki/Mostapha_al-Turk "Mostapha al-Turk") at [UFC 99](/wiki/UFC_99 "UFC 99") until the UFC pulled Duffee from the fight. Duffee was replaced by [Mirko Filipović](/wiki/Mirko_Filipovi%C4%87 "Mirko Filipović").[[8]](#cite_note-UFC_102_Complete-8)

He was next announced by the UFC to face fellow UFC newcomer [Mike Russow](/wiki/Mike_Russow "Mike Russow").[[8]](#cite_note-UFC_102_Complete-8) However, Duffee was then pulled from that match and instead was matched up against [Tim Hague](/wiki/Tim_Hague "Tim Hague") at [UFC 102](/wiki/UFC_102 "UFC 102").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Duffee set the record for the fastest KO in UFC heavyweight history (0:07) after flooring Hague with a stiff jab and rendering him unconscious with follow-up [ground and pound](/wiki/Ground-and-pound "Ground-and-pound").[[10]](#cite_note-10) After the finish, he celebrated to the camera by saying "That was an appetizer, I want to eat now [Dana](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White"), let me eat!"

Duffee was expected to face [Paul Buentello](/wiki/Paul_Buentello "Paul Buentello") at [UFC 107](/wiki/UFC_107 "UFC 107")[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) but pulled out due to a back injury.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Duffee fought [Mike Russow](/wiki/Mike_Russow "Mike Russow") at [UFC 114](/wiki/UFC_114 "UFC 114") in which he lost via KO in the third round. Prior to the fight Duffee went on record to say that he was being overhyped by the MMA world, while his opponent was very much underrated.[[14]](#cite_note-14) After dominating the first two rounds by utilizing his boxing, Duffee looked to be on his way to a unanimous decision win, when, from out of nowhere, Russow caught Duffee with two straight right hands followed by a hammerfist that prompted the referee to stop the fight as Duffee laid unconscious on the canvas. Joe Rogan stated that the nature of the knockout was "like something out of a movie" and was "one of the greatest comebacks in UFC history". Russow broke his left ulna while deflecting Duffee's punches at the end of round 1.[[15]](#cite_note-15) After the fight it was revealed that Duffee fought with a torn MCL as well as Shingles of the eye.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Duffee was expected to face [Jon Madsen](/wiki/Jon_Madsen "Jon Madsen") on October 23, 2010, at [UFC 121](/wiki/UFC_121 "UFC 121"),[[16]](#cite_note-16) but Duffee was once again forced off the card due to injury and replaced by [Gilbert Yvel](/wiki/Gilbert_Yvel "Gilbert Yvel").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

On September 7, 2010, Duffee was surprisingly released from the UFC for unknown reasons.[[18]](#cite_note-18) It is rumored that Duffee requested financial aid in order to pay for a knee surgery which he couldn't afford, but when UFC said that he must pay for it himself there was a dispute between the two parties.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Duffee spoke publicly about MMA fighters being underpaid and how he needed to take a second job just to pay for his training. Dana White later said in an interview with Ariel Helwani that Duffee had an attitude problem and that it seemed to him that "he doesn't want to be in the UFC, he doesn't like being in the UFC."[[19]](#cite_note-mmaw10-19) He also said that he could work his way back into the organization via fighting in smaller leagues. Following his release Duffee said that he couldn't understand his release and has never been told why, although he harbors no bitterness toward the UFC president or the company.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### DREAM

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: DREAM")]

After his release from the UFC, Duffee Went to [DREAM](/wiki/Dream_(mixed_martial_arts) "Dream (mixed martial arts)"), a promotion based out of Japan. Being unable to secure an opponent for former [Strikeforce Heavyweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_Strikeforce_champions#Heavyweight_Championship "List of Strikeforce champions") [Alistair Overeem](/wiki/Alistair_Overeem "Alistair Overeem") in DREAM's first Heavyweight Championship bout at [Dynamite!!](/wiki/Dynamite!!_2010 "Dynamite!! 2010"), Duffee had the manager throw his name in the mix of potentials for the bout. Less than one week before the event, he was officially named to take on Overeem in the title fight, leaving him very little time to prepare, but an opportunity to fight a big name in the sport.[[21]](#cite_note-21) As the bout started Duffee charged in very aggressively flurrying punches to no avail, but the veteran Overeem stayed calm and landed a knee that rocked Duffee, followed by punches that put him out cold at 19 seconds of the first round.[[22]](#cite_note-22) Duffee said after the fight that he respected Overeem's ability but felt that if he had sufficient time to train and prepare that it would have been a completely different outcome. Overeem brought Duffee in for his training camp in preparation for his UFC debut vs. [Brock Lesnar](/wiki/Brock_Lesnar "Brock Lesnar") in late 2011 in order for Duffee to help him with his takedown defense.[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Duffee was scheduled to face Nick Gaston on July 16, 2011, at [DREAM 17](/wiki/Dream_17 "Dream 17"),[[25]](#cite_note-25) but an injury sustained fighting Tyler East two weeks prior to fighting Duffee forced Nick Gaston to withdraw from the event. Dream was unable to find a replacement.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

### Super Fight League

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Super Fight League")]

In February 2012 Duffee signed an exclusive four-fight deal with [Super Fight League](/wiki/Super_Fight_League "Super Fight League"), India's first professional Mixed Martial Arts fighting league launched and owned by [Raj Kundra](/wiki/Raj_Kundra "Raj Kundra") & [Sanjay Dutt](/wiki/Sanjay_Dutt "Sanjay Dutt").[[27]](#cite_note-27) He faced fellow UFC veteran [Neil Grove](/wiki/Neil_Grove "Neil Grove") on the SFL's second show held on April 7, 2012.[[28]](#cite_note-28) He won via TKO after just 34 seconds in the first round, following up a right-hook that dropped Grove with heavy ground-and-pound.[[29]](#cite_note-29) After the fight he thanked his training partners at the American Kickboxing Academy and credited them with helping him through a rough training camp, and stated that he is looking to fight again as soon as possible, adding that he "missed this". Shortly after his win, with SFL taking a 4-month break until their next event it was revealed that Duffee would be allowed to fight outside of the organization that summer if he could find fights. Duffee was appealing to promoters on Twitter for fights in both MMA and Professional Boxing but none came to fruition by the time of SFL's next event.

Duffee was originally announced to fight at [SFL 4](/wiki/2012_in_Super_Fight_League#SFL4 "2012 in Super Fight League") on September 29 against former UFC title contender [Jeff Monson](/wiki/Jeff_Monson "Jeff Monson") but after the event was pushed back twice the SFL decided to change the structure of their shows, with weekly events replacing monthly events. Duffee and SFL agreed on his departure in September and he returned to free agency. Though frustrated by his fight with Monson falling out, the departure was amicable.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

### Return to UFC

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Return to UFC")]

Duffee made his UFC return against [Phil De Fries](/wiki/Phil_De_Fries "Phil De Fries") on December 29, 2012, at [UFC 155](/wiki/UFC_155 "UFC 155"), as a replacement for [Matt Mitrione](/wiki/Matt_Mitrione "Matt Mitrione").[[31]](#cite_note-31) Despite fighting with a respiratory infection he won the fight via first-round TKO, weathering an early ground exchange before finishing De Fries on the feet.[[32]](#cite_note-32) The performance earned Duffee *Knockout of the Night* honors.[[33]](#cite_note-ufc155b-33)

#### Injury, Parsonage–Turner syndrome diagnosis and return

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Injury, Parsonage–Turner syndrome diagnosis and return")]

After the De Fries fight it emerged that Duffee had fought with an undisclosed injury sustained during his training camp and would be ruled out for much of 2013. He returned to training after successfully rehabbing the injury in September. However, 3 weeks into his return to the gym, after waking up with severe pains and numbness in his arm Duffee was diagnosed with an unknown affliction which was later revealed to be [Parsonage–Turner syndrome](/wiki/Parsonage%E2%80%93Turner_syndrome "Parsonage–Turner syndrome"). Despite initially being told by doctors that he would not be able to return for at least two years, Duffee returned to training after six months and in September 2014 announced that he was ready to fight.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

Duffee returned from his extended hiatus to face [Anthony Hamilton](/wiki/Anthony_Hamilton_(fighter) "Anthony Hamilton (fighter)") on December 6, 2014, at [UFC 181](/wiki/UFC_181 "UFC 181").[[35]](#cite_note-35) He won the fight via knockout in the first round.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

Duffee faced [Frank Mir](/wiki/Frank_Mir "Frank Mir") in the main event at [UFC Fight Night 71](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_71 "UFC Fight Night 71") on July 15, 2015.[[37]](#cite_note-37) After rocking Mir with a punch, Duffee lost the fight via knockout in round one.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

Duffee was expected to face [Mark Godbeer](/wiki/Mark_Godbeer "Mark Godbeer") on March 4, 2017, at [UFC 209](/wiki/UFC_209 "UFC 209").[[39]](#cite_note-39) However, Duffee pulled out of the fight in mid-February for undisclosed reasons, later revealed to be a shoulder injury.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

After suffering setbacks in his shoulder rehabilitation, but ultimately recovering from the injury and returning to training, Duffee then suffered a knee injury. In August 2018, during the surgery to repair his ACL, the doctors discovered that he needed a surgery in his other knee as well. Subsequently, in December 2018, he underwent another surgery for the other knee and during the recovery, was infected with [MRSA](/wiki/MRSA "MRSA"). After battling the infection, Duffee relocated to [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas") in order to rehabilitate and continue training at the UFC Performance Institute.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

### Return to competition after shoulder rehabilitation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Return to competition after shoulder rehabilitation")]

After a four-year hiatus, Duffee returned to face [Jeff Hughes](/wiki/Jeff_Hughes_(fighter) "Jeff Hughes (fighter)") on September 14, 2019, at [UFC Fight Night 158](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_158 "UFC Fight Night 158").[[42]](#cite_note-42) The bout ended in the first round as a No Contest due to an accidental eye poke that rendered Duffee unable to continue.[[43]](#cite_note-43)

In May 2021, Duffee returned to training after successfully rehabbing further shoulder and knee surgeries, as well as a positive [COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19") diagnosis.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

### Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki")]

After a three-year layoff, Duffee signed with [Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki](/wiki/Konfrontacja_Sztuk_Walki "Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki") and rematched [Phil De Fries](/wiki/Phil_De_Fries "Phil De Fries") for the [KSW Heavyweight Championship](/wiki/Konfrontacja_Sztuk_Walki#Heavyweight_championship "Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki") on February 25, 2023, at [KSW 79](/wiki/2023_in_Konfrontacja_Sztuk_Walki#KSW_79:_De_Fries_vs._Duffee "2023 in Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki").[[45]](#cite_note-45) Duffee lost the bout in the first round via ground and pound TKO.[[46]](#cite_note-46)

### Rizin Fighting Federation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Rizin Fighting Federation")]

Duffee was to face former sumo wrestler [Tsuyoshi Sudario](/wiki/Tsuyoshi_Sudario "Tsuyoshi Sudario") at [Rizin 44](/wiki/2023_in_Rizin_Fighting_Federation#Rizin_44 "2023 in Rizin Fighting Federation") on September 23, 2023. However, Duffee withdrew from the fight due to a problem with his passport.[[47]](#cite_note-47)

### Global Fight League

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Global Fight League")]

Duffee was scheduled to face [Robelis Despaigne](/wiki/Robelis_Despaigne "Robelis Despaigne") in the inaugural Global Fight League event on May 24, 2025, at GFL 1.[[48]](#cite_note-48) However, all GFL events were cancelled indefinitely.[[49]](#cite_note-49)

## Bare-knuckle boxing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Bare-knuckle boxing")]

### Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship")]

Duffee was scheduled to make his bare-knuckle boxing debut for [Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship](/wiki/Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship "Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship") against [Ben Rothwell](/wiki/Ben_Rothwell "Ben Rothwell") at BKFC 56 on December 2, 2023. However, on November 30, it was announced the bout was cancelled due to Rothwell having an illness.[[50]](#cite_note-50) The fight against Rothwell has been rescheduled for [BKFC Knucklemania IV](/wiki/2024_in_Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#BKFC_Knucklemania_IV "2024 in Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship") in Los Angeles on April 27, 2024.[[51]](#cite_note-51) Duffee lost the fight by technical knockout after being knocked down and being injured in the first round.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

## Acting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Acting")]

In 2011 Duffee made his film debut in the [Michael Jai White](/wiki/Michael_Jai_White "Michael Jai White")-produced *[Never Back Down 2: The Beatdown](/wiki/Never_Back_Down_2:_The_Beatdown "Never Back Down 2: The Beatdown")*, portraying Tim Newhouse, a talented MMA fighter who lives in relative poverty and has to deal with losing his father. He also had a cameo in Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice in a fight scene.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

### Mixed martial arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Todd_Duffee&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts")]

* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + [Tied third fastest knockout in UFC history](/wiki/List_of_UFC_records#Fastest_knockouts_-_All_fighters "List of UFC records") (seven seconds)
  + [Fastest knockout in UFC Heavyweight division history](/wiki/List_of_UFC_records#Fastest_knockouts_-_By_division "List of UFC records") (seven seconds)
  + [Knockout of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (One time) vs. [Philip De Fries](/wiki/Philip_De_Fries "Philip De Fries")[[33]](#cite_note-ufc155b-33)
  + **UFC.com Awards**
    - 2009: [Ranked #10 Newcomer of the Year](/wiki/2009_in_UFC#2009_UFC.com_awards "2009 in UFC") (Tied with [Kimbo Slice](/wiki/Kimbo_Slice "Kimbo Slice") & [Yoshihiro Akiyama](/wiki/Yoshihiro_Akiyama "Yoshihiro Akiyama"))[[53]](#cite_note-53) & [Ranked #10 Knockout of the Year](/wiki/2009_in_UFC#2009_UFC.com_awards "2009 in UFC") vs. [Tim Hague](/wiki/Tim_Hague "Tim Hague")[[54]](#cite_note-54)
